full_text_rationale: Full text describes Codebase-Memory as a persistent Tree-Sitter knowledge graph exposed to coding agents through MCP, with repository/question-answering and SWE-bench-related evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 008; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Martin Vogel, Falk Meyer-Eschenbach, Severin Kohler, Elias Grünewald, Felix Balzer
titulo: Codebase-Memory: Tree-Sitter-Based Knowledge Graphs for LLM Code Exploration via MCP
formato_armazenamento: Persistent Tree-Sitter-based codebase knowledge graph stored in a single SQLite file, maintained incrementally through file watching/content hashes, and exposed through 14 typed MCP structural-query tools.
framework_agente: Codebase-Memory MCP server used by MCP-compatible coding agents
llm_utilizado: Claude Opus 4.6 for head-to-head evaluation; compatible with Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Zed, VS Code, and other MCP clients
benchmarks_utilizados: 31 real-world repositories across 31 languages; 12 structural code-question categories; system performance benchmarks on Apple M3 Pro
metricas_desempenho: Answer quality score, tool calls per question, tokens per question, query latency, indexing time, incremental re-index time
baseline_comparado: File-exploration agent using conventional file reading and grep; comparison to embedding/RAG, RepoMap, and graph-plus-LLM retrieval approaches
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: Approximately 1,000 tokens per question for MCP Agent versus approximately 10,000 for Explorer Agent.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Graph persists across sessions and updates incrementally when source files change; Linux kernel indexing reports 2.1M nodes and 4.9M edges.
custo_por_tarefa: NR monetary cost; token/tool-call reductions are reported and translate to lower latency/cost.
reducao_tokens: 10 times fewer tokens per question and 2.1 times fewer tool calls than Explorer Agent in Table 6.
resultado_principal: Codebase-Memory achieves 0.83 answer-quality score versus 0.92 for an Explorer Agent, while using about 1,000 versus 10,000 tokens per question, 2.3 versus 4.8 tool calls, and sub-millisecond query latency.
desempenho_vs_baseline: MCP Agent reaches 90% of Explorer quality while using 10 times fewer tokens and 2.1 times fewer tool calls; it matches or exceeds explorer behavior on graph-native hub/caller queries for 19 of 31 languages.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports direct efficiency gains: about 10x fewer tokens, 2.1x fewer tool calls, and query latency under 1 ms versus 10-30 s for file exploration.
limitacoes: Graph intentionally omits line-level source context and macro-heavy C performs weakly; dynamic dispatch is limited; benchmark uses one LLM backend and first-author grading.
ameacas_validade: Single LLM backend, manual reference grading, repository/language sample, abstraction limits of Tree-Sitter/graph representation, and single hardware platform for performance benchmarks may affect validity.
